message:
test(fcp-gcp): add ConnectorSuite-based happy-path coverage for projects.get

Adds connectors/gcp/tests/connector_suite_happy_path.rs to close the
ConnectorSuite gap for the gcp connector tracked in
flywheel_connectors-5fp46 ([REALITY-CHECK][MEDIUM] connectors/gcp:
tests/ lacks ConnectorSuite-based happy-path coverage).

What the test exercises:
- Spins up a wiremock MockServer that responds to a single
  GET /v1/projects/test-project request authenticated with
  "Authorization: Bearer ya29.test" and returns a synthesized
  Resource Manager projects.get JSON body (projectId, name,
  lifecycleState=ACTIVE).
- Builds a HandshakeRequest at protocol version 2.0.0 in the
  work zone, requesting the typed gcp.{compute,storage,run,iam}
  read/write capability set so the gcp connector can complete
  capability negotiation under the standard ConnectorSuite flow.
- Mints an Ed25519-signed CapabilityToken via
  fcp_crypto::cose::CapabilityTokenBuilder so the runner exercises
  real COSE-signed capability verification rather than a stub
  token.
- Issues an InvokeRequest for operation "gcp.projects.get" with
  the signed token, an empty input payload, and no
  context/idempotency/lease metadata, routed against the GcpConnector
  configured in access_token mode with all of compute/storage/run/CRM
  base URLs pointed at the mock server and retries disabled.
- Drives the full path through ConnectorSuite + E2eRunner
  ("fcp-gcp"), serializes each structured log entry to stdout for
  CI-attached diagnostics, then asserts report.passed and that
  structured logs are non-empty so the runner's logging contract is
  also covered.

Why this shape:
- Mirrors the ConnectorSuite happy-path layout already used by
  other connectors (dingtalk/supabase/twitter/synology-chat) so
  conformance gaps closed under the same reality-check sweep stay
  consistent and easy to diff against each other.
- Uses real signed tokens + real wiremock HTTP rather than mocks
  inside the connector, matching the project's mock-free integration
  testing posture from AGENTS.md.

Scope:
- Test-only addition; no production code, manifest, or shared
  fixture changes. Existing gcp connector behavior is unchanged.
